steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content: The three interior angles of any triangle sum to $180^{\circ}$. We have $\angle X = 23^{\circ}$ and $\angle Y = 66^{\circ}$, so we need to find $\angle Z$.

steps.title: Solve for angle Z
steps.content:
Set up the equation: $$23^{\circ} + 66^{\circ} + \angle Z = 180^{\circ}$$
Combine the known angles: $$89^{\circ} + \angle Z = 180^{\circ}$$
Subtract $89^{\circ}$ from both sides: $$\angle Z = 91^{\circ}$$
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
